statement.informal: Addition is not 1-node computable in the F16 family. No single F-operator equals `(+)` pointwise — established by ruling out each of the 16 candidates individually.
statement.formal_lean:
theorem add_not_one_node_computable :
    ¬ one_node_computable (· + ·) := by
  intro ⟨op, hmem, heq⟩
  have hbad : ∀ x y : ℝ, op x y = x + y := fun x y => (heq x y).symm
  exact no_f16_computes_add op hmem hbad

tactics: have hbad : ∀ x y : ℝ, op x y = x + y := fun x y => (heq x y).symm

common_mistakes.why_fails: Writing just `intro h` and then needing `obtain` separately — works, more verbose.
Trying `rw [Eq.comm] at heq` — works but is heavier than the direct `.symm`.
Forgetting the symmetric flip — the downstream lemma `no_f16_computes_add` expects `op x y = x + y`, not `x + y = op x y`.
Trying to re-prove the case analysis inline — would just duplicate `no_f16_computes_add`'s body.
